title: Bitter Crunchy German Duck Toast
description: Roasted duck legs rest atop toasted rye bread, accented with pickled beetroot, horseradish‑mustard sauce, and crunchy almond slivers for a satisfying, diabetic‑friendly German crowd‑pleaser.

ingredients.name: duck legs
ingredients.notes: skin on, bones removed after roasting

instructions: Pat the duck legs dry, season with salt, pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil, then place skin side up on a baking sheet.
Roast the duck legs for 35 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 74 °C (165 °F). Let rest 5 minutes before slicing.
While the duck roasts, heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat; add a splash of olive oil and toast each bread slice until golden brown, about 2 minutes per side.
Warm the pickled beetroot slices in a small saucepan over low heat for 2 minutes.
In a bowl, whisk together whole‑grain mustard, grated horseradish, fresh dill, and lemon juice to form the sauce.
Place a toasted bread slice on a plate, top with warm beetroot, then drizzle with the horseradish‑mustard sauce.
Slice the rested duck leg into medallions and arrange them over the beetroot‑topped toast.
Sprinkle toasted almond slivers on top for crunch and extra protein.
Serve immediately while the toast is still crisp and the duck is warm.
